title: salix purpurea var. vitellina
intro: Salix purpurea var. vitellina, also known as golden willow, is a variety of willow tree that is native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its vibrant yellow bark and slender branches. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its salicin content. The health benefits of golden willow include reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as acting as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. Golden willow can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
